samples.xml_description:
<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer with a 3-clock cycle latency. The input data remains constant during the period when data_en is high, and data_en is high for at least 3 clk_b clock cycles. The clock clk_a is responsible for the input data and valid_count signals, while clk_b is responsible for the data_en signal, which is delayed by two cycles compared to clk_a. The input data is synchronized when valid_count is high, and the data_en signal is used to control the synchronization.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B.</input>
        <input id="rstn">Active-low reset.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input data enable.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="enum_data_reg">
            <enum_value id="initial_value" value="0">Initial value of the data register.</enum_value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="data_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[3:0]">
            <description>Register to store input data.</description>
            <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A.</input>
            <input id="arstn">Active-low reset.</input>
            <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data.</input>
            <output id="value" width_description="[3:0]">Stored input data.</output>
        </reg>
        <reg id="en_data_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store data enable.</description>
            <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A.</input>
            <input id="arstn">Active-low reset.</description>
            <input id="data_en">Input data enable.</input>
            <output id="value">Stored data enable.</output>
        </reg>
        <reg id="en_clap_one" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>First control register.</description>
            <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B.</input>
            <input id="brstn">Active-low reset.</input>
            <input id="en_data_reg">Input data enable.</input>
            <output id="value">Output of the first control register.</output>
        </reg>
        <reg id="en_clap_two" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Second control register.</description>
            <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B.</input>
            <input id="brstn">Active-low reset.</input>
            <input id="en_clap_one">Input of the second control register.</input>
            <output id="value">Output of the second control register.</output>
        </reg>
        <reg id="dataout" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Output register.</description>
            <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B.</input>
            <input id="brstn">Active-low reset.</input>
            <input id="en_clap_two">Control signal.</input>
            <input id="data_reg" width_description="[3:0]">Input data.</input>
            <output id="value" width_description="[3:0]">Output data.</output>
        </reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module includes a register, data_reg, to store the input data signal, data_in. On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register. If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the register (data_reg) is set to 0. If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (data_reg) is updated with the input data signal (data_in).
    The module includes a register, en_data_reg, to store the input enable signal, data_en. On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register. If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, the register (en_data_reg) is set to 0. If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (en_data_reg) is updated with the input enable signal (data_en).
    The module includes two registers, en_clap_one and en_clap_two, to control the selection of the input data. On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module updates the registers. If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, both registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are set to 0. If the reset signal B (brstn) is high, the registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are updated based on the value of en_data_reg. The register en_clap_one is assigned the value of en_data_reg, and en_clap_two is assigned the previous value of en_clap_one.
    On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module assigns the output data value. If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the output data (dataout) is set to 0. If the reset signal B (brstn) is high and the control signal (en_clap_two) is active, the output data (dataout) is assigned the value of the data register (data_reg). If the control signal (en_clap_two) is inactive, the output data (dataout) retains its previous value.
    </implementation>
</module>
